title: Why Europe Is Unlikely to Face an Inflation Surge
content: In 2021-22, the European Central Bank failed to pay sufficient heed to mounting inflationary pressures, leading to a delayed response that made matters much worse. While today’s energy-price shock remains less acute than the one caused by Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine, the ECB appears committed to a more proactive response.
snippet: In 2021-22, the European Central Bank failed to pay sufficient heed to mounting inflationary pressures, leading to a delayed response that made matters much worse. While today’s energy-price shock rem
creator: Daniel Gros
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Can China Grow From Within?
content: At a time of intensifying geopolitical volatility, China’s embrace of a consumption-led model is not only about rebalancing growth, but also about anchoring it more firmly at home. Domestic demand offers insulation from external shocks, and along with developed capital markets, it can go a long way toward strengthening autonomy.
snippet: At a time of intensifying geopolitical volatility, China’s embrace of a consumption-led model is not only about rebalancing growth, but also about anchoring it more firmly at home. Domestic demand off
creator: Keyu Jin
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will Kharg Island Decide the Future of US Alliances?
content: As confidence in the United States has eroded, allies have begun to hedge their bets by not automatically aligning themselves with America in the face of new crises. The US-Israeli war on Iran has thrown this dynamic into sharp relief, revealing a fundamental new constraint on American power.
snippet: As confidence in the United States has eroded, allies have begun to hedge their bets by not automatically aligning themselves with America in the face of new crises. The US-Israeli war on Iran has thr
creator: Carla Norrlöf
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why America, Not Iran, Has a Succession Problem
content: The United States is fighting Iran—a state that built institutional continuity into its founding architecture—with strategic assumptions borrowed from more personalistic regimes, where decapitation can work. Ironically, under President Donald Trump, the US is reshaping its own political system in that direction.
snippet: The United States is fighting Iran—a state that built institutional continuity into its founding architecture—with strategic assumptions borrowed from more personalistic regimes, where decapitation ca
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cuba in Free Fall
content: In the space of just a few weeks, Cuba’s external energy supply and main sources of foreign earnings have been cut off. With economic conditions rapidly deteriorating and many industries grinding to a halt, social unrest is mounting, posing a potential threat to the island’s governability.
snippet: In the space of just a few weeks, Cuba’s external energy supply and main sources of foreign earnings have been cut off. With economic conditions rapidly deteriorating and many industries grinding to a
creator: Pavel Vidal
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Wars Fought for Fun Cannot Be Won
content: Many commentators have tried to divine a policy justification for the US war in Iran. But the simple explanation is that US President Donald Trump and US Secretary of “War” (Defense) Pete Hegseth attacked the Islamic Republic because they could, and because they take pleasure in killing or dominating other people.
snippet: Many commentators have tried to divine a policy justification for the US war in Iran. But the simple explanation is that US President Donald Trump and US Secretary of “War” (Defense) Pete Hegseth atta
creator: Timothy Snyder
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Cuba’s Third Chance
content: While the ongoing US oil blockade has exacerbated Cuba’s economic and humanitarian crisis, the primary responsibility for its current predicament lies with the communist regime. If Cubans use this crisis as an opportunity to pursue ambitious economic and political reforms, they may yet restore the prosperity they once knew.
snippet: While the ongoing US oil blockade has exacerbated Cuba’s economic and humanitarian crisis, the primary responsibility for its current predicament lies with the communist regime. If Cubans use this cri
creator: Enrique Krauze
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: International Law Is Still Worth Defending
content: While a world where power trumps principle is hostile to small states, it is also bad for great powers. The challenges that will shape our future do not respect borders or recognize spheres of influence, and they can be contained only through binding agreements and institutionalized cooperation.
snippet: While a world where power trumps principle is hostile to small states, it is also bad for great powers. The challenges that will shape our future do not respect borders or recognize spheres of influen
creator: Andreas Motzfeldt Kravik
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Building the Middle-Power World Order
content: If Canada and other "middle powers" are serious about building a different kind of global order based on "institutions and agreements that function as described," the time to start planning is now. Only with a clear vision can they hope to make a difference when the next big crisis forces a search for alternatives.
snippet: If Canada and other "middle powers" are serious about building a different kind of global order based on "institutions and agreements that function as described," the time to start planning is now. On
creator: Anne-Marie Slaughter
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Nuclear Deterrence Is No Longer Enough
content: Today, violent conflicts are increasingly interconnected, with each new hotspot amplifying others and increasing the strain on the system as a whole. Under such conditions, wars no longer remain separate but can converge into a single strategic crisis – all while remaining below the nuclear threshold.
snippet: Today, violent conflicts are increasingly interconnected, with each new hotspot amplifying others and increasing the strain on the system as a whole. Under such conditions, wars no longer remain separ
creator: Antony Dabila
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Independent Journalism Finds a Way
content: Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has sought to tame Hungary’s independent media outlets through regulatory engineering, financial pressure, and ownership concentration. But he never fully defeated them, and their reporting on the regime’s corruption and abuses of power has helped fuel the opposition’s rise ahead of April’s election.
snippet: Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has sought to tame Hungary’s independent media outlets through regulatory engineering, financial pressure, and ownership concentration. But he never fully defeated them, an
creator: Ákos Tóth
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Scars in Africa-Europe Relations
content: Relations between Africa and Europe continue to be shaped – and distorted – by the historical trauma of five centuries of slavery and colonialism. Donald Trump’s return to the White House has exposed European leaders’ lack of confidence and moral fiber, as well as their unwillingness to treat Africa as an equal.
snippet: Relations between Africa and Europe continue to be shaped – and distorted – by the historical trauma of five centuries of slavery and colonialism. Donald Trump’s return to the White House has exposed
creator: Adekeye Adebajo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America Should Beware of Economic Hubris
content: Even if the US economy continues outperforming its peers, it will not necessarily remain insulated from the Iran war’s adverse spillovers. Already, higher energy and borrowing costs are exacerbating the affordability pressures many Americans face, creating downside risks for jobs, consumption, and growth.
snippet: Even if the US economy continues outperforming its peers, it will not necessarily remain insulated from the Iran war’s adverse spillovers. Already, higher energy and borrowing costs are exacerbating t
creator: Mohamed A. El-Erian
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Remaking Europe’s Energy System for the Age of AI
content: Europe will never have the abundant fossil-fuel resources of the US, but it can still achieve the kinds of energy diversification and cost reductions seen in China. This would protect Europe from price spikes and enable the EU to compete in the defining economic race of our time: turning electricity into intelligence.
snippet: Europe will never have the abundant fossil-fuel resources of the US, but it can still achieve the kinds of energy diversification and cost reductions seen in China. This would protect Europe from pric
creator: Lucrezia Reichlin
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Iran’s Escalation Strategy Is Likely to Backfire
content: Iran’s current strategy in the war with the United States and Israel is designed to demonstrate the regime’s resilience, and to show that the region will not know peace unless Iran does. But the economic and diplomatic consequences ultimately will weigh more heavily on Iran than on its adversaries.
snippet: Iran’s current strategy in the war with the United States and Israel is designed to demonstrate the regime’s resilience, and to show that the region will not know peace unless Iran does. But the econo
creator: Mohammed Al Dhaheri
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Sad Demise of China’s Economic Debate
content: China has never been an open society where ideas can be debated freely, save for one area: economic policy. But such engagement is no longer possible, reflected in the demise of the China Development Forum, which has morphed from a platform of true give-and-take into one cheerleading session after another.
snippet: China has never been an open society where ideas can be debated freely, save for one area: economic policy. But such engagement is no longer possible, reflected in the demise of the China Development
creator: Stephen S. Roach
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Taking the Battle for Human Attention Seriously
content: Now that Meta and YouTube have been held liable by a US jury for deliberately addicting young users, leading to numerous mental health problems, it is time to stop viewing human attention as an exploitable resource. In fact, it is a collective infrastructure essential to the survival of open societies.
snippet: Now that Meta and YouTube have been held liable by a US jury for deliberately addicting young users, leading to numerous mental health problems, it is time to stop viewing human attention as an exploi
creator: Jacques Attali
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Iran’s Water Weapon Against the Gulf
content: While the rest of the world is mainly concerned about the energy disruptions caused by the Iran war, the Gulf countries are more anxious about the Islamic Republic’s threats to their desalination facilities. If the US attempts to seize Kharg Island, it could spell disaster for the region's "saltwater kingdoms."
snippet: While the rest of the world is mainly concerned about the energy disruptions caused by the Iran war, the Gulf countries are more anxious about the Islamic Republic’s threats to their desalination faci
creator: Michael Christopher Low
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: US Escalation Is the Most Likely Scenario in Iran
content: If Donald Trump walks away from the war with Iran now, the threat to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz will remain, risk premia on oil prices will stay permanently higher, and Trump’s own popularity will sink even further ahead of this year’s midterm elections. Despite the obvious risks, he has every reason to try to “finish the job.”
snippet: If Donald Trump walks away from the war with Iran now, the threat to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz will remain, risk premia on oil prices will stay permanently higher, and Trump’s own popularity wi
creator: Nouriel Roubini
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Only Boots on the Ground in Iran Should Be IAEA Inspectors
content: The only appropriate authority that can account for and monitor Iran’s nuclear stockpile is the International Atomic Energy Agency, which is legally mandated to do so under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons. No one else has the expertise or the independence to provide oversight without further escalating the conflict.
snippet: The only appropriate authority that can account for and monitor Iran’s nuclear stockpile is the International Atomic Energy Agency, which is legally mandated to do so under the Treaty on the Non-Proli
creator: Helen Clark
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page
